findings.evidence: Lines 55-56 define `chiNumerator`; lines 84-85 prove `two_mul_chi` only against `-P.chiNumerator k x`; `rg -n "theorem chiNumerator_def|theorem chi_def|chiNumerator.*intersectionForm" code/TauCeti/LowDimTopology/Plumbing/Weight.lean` finds no public general restatement besides the private proof unfold at line 104 for basis vectors.
findings.fix: Add a public characteristic theorem, or otherwise expose exactly the numerator API needed, giving `P.chiNumerator k x = k ⬝ᵥ x + P.intersectionForm x x` and/or `2 * P.chi k hk x = -(k ⬝ᵥ x + P.intersectionForm x x)` so later cube-weight code need not unfold definitions.
findings.issue: `two_mul_chi` states the doubled formula only as `2 * P.chi k hk x = -P.chiNumerator k x`, while `chiNumerator` has no exported `_def`/characterization lemma for general `x`; importers are left with an opaque helper instead of the advertised `-(k ⬝ᵥ x + P.intersectionForm x x)`.

summary: The new weight surface is still missing a public characterization of the general formula it advertises, so downstream code cannot use `χₖ(x)` in terms of the existing covector evaluation and intersection form without unfolding hidden bodies.
